jobs.commentText: this should be a gallery of photos @Ahmed Marwan

options.label: Grid Gallery
options.caption: Replaced the single Interior Image panel with a 3-up auto-layout grid of photos so the section reads as a browsable gallery.
options.aiPrompt: Inside the 'Brand Intro' frame that contains 'Interior Image', restructure it into a 3-column auto-layout grid of image tiles. Keep each tile on its own auto-layout frame with consistent gap and padding, reuse the existing local paint styles for tile backgrounds, and keep captions like 'Just For You' and 'Curated Dining' using the existing semantic text styles, one per tile.

options.label: Horizontal Filmstrip
options.caption: Converted the Brand Intro strip into a horizontally scrollable row of photos so visitors can swipe through the space instead of seeing one static image.
options.aiPrompt: Convert the 'Brand Intro' section into a horizontal auto-layout row with scroll overflow. Clone the 'Interior Image' frame for each additional photo, keep consistent auto-layout gap/padding between tiles, and retain existing captions ('Just For You', 'Curated Dining') as overlay labels using the current semantic text styles and paint styles — do not hardcode new hex or px values.

options.label: Mosaic Grid
options.caption: Built an asymmetric mosaic of large and small photo tiles for visual variety instead of a uniform grid or single image.
options.aiPrompt: Rework the 'Brand Intro' section into an asymmetric mosaic auto-layout grid (mix of large and small tiles) built from clones of the existing 'Interior Image' frame. Preserve auto layout on every tile and the parent frame, reuse existing local paint and text styles for backgrounds and captions, and keep the 'Just For You' / 'Curated Dining' labels attached to their respective photos.

jobs.commentText: this instance of a component set I like the swapping effect but I need the "enquire now" box to be in the center on the left not the bottom

options.label: Center-left via Hero Form alignment
options.caption: Change Hero Form's auto layout so its primary axis is set to Center and counter axis to Left, moving the whole Frame 17/18 stack (including the button) up from the bottom into vertical center while staying flush left.
options.aiPrompt: In the 'landing page - claude' frame, inside the Brand Intro instance, select 'Hero Form' and change its auto layout: primary axis alignment to Center, counter axis alignment to Left. This should move the ENQUIRE NOW button and its stack up from the bottom into vertical center, left-aligned.

options.label: Isolate CTA into its own left-anchored slot
options.caption: Wrap the Button instance in its own auto-layout row set to align left, independent from the Frame 18 copy stack, then set Hero Form's primary axis to Center — this moves only the button, leaving the headline/body copy where they are.
options.aiPrompt: Inside Hero Form, wrap the 'Button' instance (ENQUIRE NOW) in a new auto-layout frame with alignment set to Left. Set Hero Form's primary axis alignment to Center so this new button frame centers vertically within Hero Form without affecting the position of Frame 18's headline/body text.

options.label: Center the full content block
options.caption: Change Frame 18's parent auto layout from bottom/space-between to Center on the primary axis, keeping counter axis at Left — centers the entire copy-plus-button block, which also shifts the headline upward, not just the button.
options.aiPrompt: In Hero Form, change Frame 17's auto layout primary axis alignment from its current bottom/space-between setting to Center, keeping counter axis alignment at Left. Note this centers the whole Frame 18 stack (headline, body copy, and ENQUIRE NOW button) as one unit, not the button alone.

jobs.commentText: I need better layout of these photos @Ahmed Marwan

options.label: Uniform grid
options.caption: Aligned all four property cards (Frame 8 instances) into an even grid with consistent image proportions, trading the current staggered rhythm for scannability.
options.aiPrompt: In the 'landing page - claude' frame, take the four property card instances currently inside 'Frame 8' (Maison Blanche, The Meridian Residences, Casa Soleil, One Whitmore) and rearrange them into a uniform 2x2 auto-layout grid with equal column widths and equal image aspect ratios, consistent gutter spacing, keeping each card's existing text and button content intact.

options.label: Asymmetric masonry
options.caption: Varied card widths/heights (e.g. one large hero property, three smaller) to create visual hierarchy among the listings instead of equal-weight repetition.
options.aiPrompt: In the 'landing page - claude' frame, restyle the property cards inside 'Frame 8' into an asymmetric masonry layout — make one card (e.g. Maison Blanche) span a larger featured tile and the remaining three (The Meridian Residences, Casa Soleil, One Whitmore) sit smaller alongside it, using auto layout so the arrangement stays responsive, keeping all existing text and copy unchanged.

options.label: Horizontal scroll carousel
options.caption: Converted the stacked cards into a single-row horizontally scrollable carousel, prioritizing a compact vertical footprint over showing every property at once.
options.aiPrompt: In the 'landing page - claude' frame, convert the property cards inside 'Frame 8' (Maison Blanche, The Meridian Residences, Casa Soleil, One Whitmore) from their current stacked layout into a horizontal auto-layout row with overflow scroll behavior, so the cards read as a carousel instead of a vertical stack, preserving each card's existing image, text, and button content.

jobs.commentText: This section needs to in light mode @Ahmed Marwan

options.label: Full Light Flip
options.caption: Inverted every section in the frame — Hero + Nav, all Brand Intro blocks, Pre-Footer, and Footer — to a light background with dark type, trading the current moody/editorial contrast for a brighter, real-estate-brochure feel throughout.

ops.match: Where every stay becomes a Story
Wellth isn't just a place to stay — it's a framework for how you live. Every residence is designed to serve your wellness, your privacy, and your sense of arrival.
From intimate dinners curated to your taste, to early mornings that feel like the world belongs only to you — Wellth is designed for the moments between the milestones.
Square feet of curated living space across Mountain View's finest addresses.
Resident satisfaction score, year over year, across all Wellth properties.
Dedicated lifestyle concierge, available around the clock for every resident.

options.aiPrompt: Flip the entire 'landing page - claude' frame to light mode: swap dark section backgrounds for light neutrals, invert all body/heading text and icon colors to dark for contrast, and adjust any hero photography overlays/gradients so imagery still reads correctly against the new light background. Keep existing layout, spacing, and auto layout structure untouched.

options.label: Hero-Only Light Section
options.caption: Flipped just the Hero + Nav section to light, leaving the Brand Intro, Pre-Footer, and Footer sections in their current dark treatment — cheaper to execute and creates a light-to-dark rhythm as you scroll instead of a flat, all-light page.

ops.match: Share a few details and our concierge will be in touch.

options.aiPrompt: Flip only the Hero + Nav section of 'landing page - claude' to light mode — light background, dark text/icons for contrast — while leaving the Brand Intro, Pre-Footer, and Footer sections exactly as they are now. Keep the hero photography and form component untouched aside from any overlay needed for legibility.

options.label: Alternating Light Bands
options.caption: Lightened alternating Brand Intro blocks while keeping Hero and Footer dark, giving the page an editorial light/dark banding rhythm rather than a single hard cutover — more visual variety, but requires deciding which specific instances alternate rather than a single clean rule.

ops.match: Wellth isn't just a place to stay — it's a framework for how you live. Every residence is designed to serve your wellness, your privacy, and your sense of arrival.
Square feet of curated living space across Mountain View's finest addresses.
Resident satisfaction score, year over year, across all Wellth properties.
Dedicated lifestyle concierge, available around the clock for every resident.

options.aiPrompt: In 'landing page - claude', pick alternating Brand Intro blocks (e.g. the 1st and 3rd of the four) and flip just those to a light background with dark text, leaving the Hero, remaining Brand Intro blocks, Pre-Footer, and Footer dark, so the page reads as alternating light/dark editorial bands rather than one flat mode switch.

options.label: Minimal Editorial
options.caption: Strip the section to one dominant image with a single stacked headline and drop the secondary card, so the eye lands on one story instead of splitting between Wellness & Health and Private Dining.
options.aiPrompt: Redesign this Brand Intro section as a minimal editorial layout: one full-bleed interior image, a single stacked headline ('Indulge & Unwind') top-left, generous negative space, remove the secondary Wellness & Health / Private Dining card pairing. Keep auto layout on every frame and use only local styles.

options.label: Bold Contrast Split
options.caption: Push Wellness & Health and Private Dining into a high-contrast two-up grid with a darker ground so the section reads as a deliberate pairing rather than a default card row.
options.aiPrompt: Redesign this Brand Intro section as a bold, high-contrast two-column split: Wellness & Health on one side, Private Dining on the other, equal visual weight, darker background ground, tighter vertical rhythm. Auto layout throughout, local styles only, no hardcoded hex/px.

options.label: Sequential Storyboard
options.caption: Reflow the section into a horizontal storyboard (Indulge & Unwind → Wellness & Health → Private Dining) so it reads as a guided sequence rather than a static grid — biggest structural change, mostly a layout rebuild.
options.aiPrompt: Rebuild this Brand Intro section as a horizontal storyboard: reorder Indulge & Unwind, Wellness & Health, and Private Dining into a left-to-right sequence with connecting rail/divider elements, equal-width auto-layout panels, consistent image aspect ratios. Use only existing local paint/text styles by name, no hardcoded values, icons at 16/20/24 only if any are introduced.

jobs.commentText: I want to add another statement placed in a nice way "Mountain VIew Hospitality" @Ahmed Marwan

report.author: Ahmed Marwan
report.comment: I want to add another statement placed in a nice way "Mountain VIew Hospitality" @Ahmed Marwan
report.rationale: Clear action (add a new statement) and clear target text is quoted, matching the duplicate+setText pattern for adding another statement near the pin-anchored heading.

report.author: Ahmed Marwan
report.comment: this should be a gallery of photos @Ahmed Marwan
report.rationale: Turning a single image section into a 'gallery of photos' is a layout/visual-direction call with no specified target images, count, or arrangement — not a single mechanical op.

report.author: Ahmed Marwan
report.comment: this instance of a component set I like the swapping effect but I need the "enquire now" box to be in the center on the left not the bottom
report.rationale: The requested outcome (button centered vertically, left-aligned instead of bottom) is a layout/alignment change with no reposition or auto-layout-alignment op in the vocabulary, so achieving it requires a layout judgment call on which frame's alignment to change and what else shifts as a result.
options: Isolate CTA into its own left-anchored slot

report.author: Ahmed Marwan
report.comment: I need better layout of these photos @Ahmed Marwan
report.rationale: "Better layout of these photos" gives no concrete target or action — it's a visual-direction call, not a mechanical edit.

report.author: Ahmed Marwan
report.comment: This section needs to in light mode @Ahmed Marwan
report.rationale: "Needs to be in light mode" asks for a visual-direction flip with no spec on which sections or how far to take it, so it calls for judgment, not a single mechanical swap.

report.author: Ahmed Marwan
report.rationale: 'I want this section redesigned' gives no visual direction, target style, or scope — needs judgment calls, not a mechanical op.

report.author: Ahmed Marwan
report.comment: and this black gradient overlay should follow the same style as the section below @Ahmed Marwan
report.rationale: No node named for the gradient overlay exists in this frame's inventory and 'the section below' isn't identified, so there's no clear target or reference to execute against.
report.reply: I looked through this frame but couldn't find a layer for the black gradient overlay, and I wasn't sure which section below you meant as the reference. Could you point me to the overlay node and the specific section whose style I should match? I didn't want to guess and end up matching the wrong one.

report.author: Ahmed Marwan
report.comment: I like the effect but I need the photos to be a little bit bigger @Ahmed Marwan
report.rationale: No op in the vocabulary can resize/scale a node, and 'the photos' plausibly refers to several different image sets on this frame (hero, portfolio cards near the pin), so neither the action nor the target can be executed as a mechanical edit without more specifics.
report.reply: Good note — I agreed the photos could come up in size, but I paused before touching anything. Which set did I mean: the property card images in the portfolio section by Frame 8, or the hero imagery up top? And roughly how much bigger — a size bump there ate into the padding around the card text, so I wanted to confirm the amount before I threw off that rhythm.
